---
title: "Three-Dataset Bias Mitigation in AI Hiring"
subtitle: "A DALC/MLOps Approach in R"
author: ""
date: "2026"
output: ioslides_presentation
---

## Problem Statement

- AI hiring tools deployed globally to screen candidates
- Risk: models encode **historical biases** from training data
- Amazon (2018): hiring tool penalized female applicants
- High-stakes decisions demand **fairness + transparency**

**Research Questions:**

1. Does the baseline model exhibit measurable gender bias?
2. Can reweighting reduce bias while maintaining performance?
3. Does the pipeline generalize across three different datasets?

## Motivation: Why This Matters in 2026

- Increasing regulatory pressure (EU AI Act, UAE AI Strategy 2031)
- Organizations need **auditable, explainable** hiring AI
- Fairness is not optional — it is a **compliance requirement**
- Single-dataset studies lack credibility for real-world adoption
- **Three-dataset validation** strengthens trust and generalizability

## Literature Foundations

| Area | Key Work | Contribution |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Fairness theory | Barocas & Hardt [5] | Fairness definitions & impossibility |
| Reweighting | Kamiran & Calders [10] | Inverse-frequency pre-processing |
| Impossibility | Chouldechova [8] | Calibration vs. error rate balance |
| Toolkit | Bellamy et al. [7] | IBM AI Fairness 360 |
| Hiring bias | Amazon/Reuters [4] | Real-world gender discrimination |
| XAI | DALEX framework | Model-agnostic explanations |

## DALC/MLOps Methodology: 6-Phase Pipeline

1. **Data Preparation** — Load, clean, encode, 70/15/15 stratified split
2. **EDA & Bias Detection** — Selection rates, distributions, DI ratio
3. **Modeling + Reweighting** — Baseline RF + 3 mitigated models per dataset
4. **Fairness Evaluation** — DPD, EOD, DIR, Calibration metrics
5. **XAI Explainability** — Feature importance, break-down, PDP, ROC
6. **Deployment** — Plumber REST API + Shiny Dashboard

## Dataset 1: Synthetic Hiring Data

**`final_bias_hiring_dataset.csv`** — 2,000 applicants

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Records | 2,000 |
| Features (modeled) | 7 |
| Gender split | 991 Female / 1,009 Male |
| Selection rate | 57.8% |
| Disparate impact ratio | 0.980 |

- Near-balanced gender representation
- Designed for controlled bias injection experiments

## Dataset 2: Kaggle AI Hiring Bias Detection

**`data.csv`** — 1,500 candidates

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Records | 1,500 |
| Features (modeled) | 11 |
| Gender split | 762 Female / 738 Male |
| Hiring rate | 31.0% |
| Disparate impact ratio | 0.993 |

- More selective scenario (31% vs. 58% positive rate)
- Naturalistic validation of pipeline generalizability

## Dataset 3: FairJob Dataset

**`job fair dataset.csv`** — up to 2,000 candidates

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Records | Up to 2,000 (filtered) |
| Features (modeled) | 9 |
| Target | Employed (0/1) |
| Protected attribute | Gender (Man/Woman) |

- Employment outcome data from FairJob challenge
- Features: Age, Education, Mental Health, Coding Years, Previous Salary, Computer Skills
- Highest baseline bias among all three datasets

## EDA Findings: Gender Hiring Rates

**Dataset 1:** Female 58.3% | Male 57.2% — DI = 0.980

**Dataset 2:** Female 31.1% | Male 30.9% — DI = 0.993

**Dataset 3:** Exhibits highest gender disparity in employment outcomes

**Key insight:** Raw data may appear balanced, but model predictions can exhibit bias through feature interactions

## Reweighting Strategy

Inverse-frequency weights by (Gender, Outcome):

$$w_{g,y} = \frac{N}{|G| \times n_{g,y}}$$

- **Random Forest**: case weights applied via tidymodels `add_case_weights()`
- **Naive Bayes & KNN**: weighted resampling (sample with replacement)

**4 models per dataset:** Baseline RF, Mitigated RF, Mitigated NB, Mitigated KNN

## Model Performance: Dataset 1

| Model | Accuracy | AUC | F1 |
|-------|----------|-----|-----|
| Baseline RF | 0.565 | 0.494 | 0.675 |
| Mitigated RF | 0.508 | 0.489 | 0.584 |
| Mitigated NB | 0.548 | 0.519 | 0.595 |
| Mitigated KNN | 0.505 | 0.453 | 0.536 |

- NB achieves best AUC (0.519) among all Dataset 1 models
- Low absolute AUC reflects near-random separability in synthetic data

## Model Performance: Dataset 2

| Model | Accuracy | AUC | F1 |
|-------|----------|-----|-----|
| Baseline RF | 0.889 | 0.911 | 0.790 |
| **Mitigated RF** | **0.894** | **0.916** | **0.829** |
| Mitigated NB | 0.832 | 0.875 | 0.725 |
| Mitigated KNN | 0.801 | 0.819 | 0.702 |

- Mitigated RF **exceeds baseline** (0.894 vs. 0.889 accuracy)
- Fairness and performance are **not always in conflict**

## Model Performance: Dataset 3

| Model | Accuracy | AUC | F1 |
|-------|----------|-----|-----|
| Baseline RF | 0.771 | 0.856 | 0.793 |
| Mitigated RF | 0.784 | 0.865 | 0.795 |
| Mitigated NB | 0.754 | 0.869 | 0.792 |
| Mitigated KNN | 0.668 | 0.714 | 0.677 |

- Mitigated NB achieves highest AUC (0.869)
- Mitigated RF improves both accuracy and AUC over baseline

## Fairness Metrics: All Datasets

| Dataset | Model | DPD | EOD | Calib. | DIR |
|---------|-------|-----|-----|--------|-----|
| D1 | Baseline RF | 0.036 | 0.051 | 0.004 | 0.954 |
| D2 | Baseline RF | 0.111 | 0.201 | 0.008 | 0.591 |
| D2 | Mitigated NB | **0.065** | 0.222 | 0.019 | **0.804** |
| D3 | Baseline RF | 0.171 | 0.287 | 0.021 | 0.701 |
| D3 | Mitigated NB | **0.045** | **0.131** | 0.126 | **0.930** |

## Bias Reduction Summary

| Dataset | Baseline DPD | Best Mitigated DPD | Reduction |
|---------|-------------|-------------------|-----------|
| Dataset 1 | 0.036 | 0.048 (KNN) | -34.1%* |
| Dataset 2 | 0.111 | 0.065 (NB) | **41.1%** |
| Dataset 3 | 0.171 | 0.045 (NB) | **73.5%** |

*Dataset 1 baseline bias was already very low (DPD = 0.036)

**Key finding:** Naive Bayes with resampling achieves best demographic parity

## Calibration Analysis

- **Calibration metric**: measures whether predicted probabilities are equally accurate across gender groups
- Baseline RF maintains good calibration (0.004–0.021)
- Mitigated RF has competitive calibration (0.006–0.017)
- Mitigated NB trades calibration for DPD improvement on Dataset 3 (cal = 0.126)
- **Lesson**: multiple fairness metrics needed to capture full picture

## XAI: Feature Importance

**Dataset 1:** Interview Score > Skills Score > CGPA > Experience

**Dataset 2:** Interview Score > Skill Score > Experience > Personality

**Dataset 3:** Computer Skills > Previous Salary > Coding Years

**Cross-dataset:** Gender has minimal direct importance — bias operates through correlated features

## XAI: Attribution & PDP

**Break-Down Attribution:**

- Individual predictions decomposed by feature contribution
- Supports "right to explanation" requirements

**Partial Dependence:**

- Interview Score and Computer Skills show monotonic positive effect
- Gender-stratified ROC curves converge after mitigation

## Deployment: API + Shiny Dashboard

**Plumber REST API:**
```
GET  /health           → Model status check
GET  /fairness_report  → Aggregate fairness metrics
POST /predict          → Prediction + probability + fairness scores
```

**Shiny Dashboard (4 tabs):**

- Model Performance — metrics comparison across datasets
- Fairness Metrics — DPD, EOD, DIR, Calibration plots
- Before/After — side-by-side baseline vs. mitigated comparison
- Predict — interactive prediction form for all 3 datasets

## UAE and Regional Relevance

- **UAE AI Strategy 2031**: ethical AI is a national priority
- **GCC workforce diversity**: expatriate hiring amplifies fairness stakes
- **Visa/residency implications**: hiring decisions have outsized impact

**Our pipeline provides:**

1. Measurable fairness metrics for **audit compliance**
2. Explainable predictions for **regulatory transparency**
3. Deployable API + dashboard for **HR tech integration**

## Limitations and Future Work

**Current limitations:**

- Synthetic data may not capture real-world complexity
- Single protected attribute (binary Gender only)
- No causal fairness claims
- Static pipeline without drift monitoring

**Future directions:**

- Multi-attribute intersectional fairness (race, age, disability)
- Post-processing calibration techniques
- Concept drift monitoring in production

## Conclusion

- **End-to-end bias mitigation pipeline** validated on three datasets
- **73.5% bias reduction** on FairJob dataset (DPD: 0.171 → 0.045)
- **Performance preserved or improved** on Dataset 2 (mitigated RF beats baseline)
- **Four fairness metrics**: DPD, EOD, DIR, and Calibration
- **Three model types**: RF, Naive Bayes, KNN with reweighting
- **Explainability** via DALEX + **Shiny dashboard** for HR teams
- **Deployable** via Plumber REST API

## References

[1] Ahmed et al., IEEE 2025 — XAI in hiring bias
[2] Patel et al., IEEE 2025 — Fairness in resume screening
[3] DLA Piper, 2025 — AI bias framework
[4] Dastin, Reuters 2018 — Amazon hiring bias
[5] Barocas & Hardt — Fairness and ML
[6] Mehrabi et al., ACM 2021 — Bias survey
[7] Bellamy et al., IBM 2019 — AI Fairness 360
[8] Chouldechova, 2017 — Impossibility results
[9] Corbett-Davies & Goel, 2018 — Fairness review
[10] Kamiran & Calders, 2012 — Reweighting
[11] Dwork et al., 2012 — Individual fairness
[12] Friedler et al., 2019 — Intervention benchmark

## Q&A

**Thank you for your attention.**

All code, data, and artifacts are available in the project repository.

Questions?
